root_text.sn55.25:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena saraṇāni sakko kālaṅkato hoti.
root_text.sn55.25:1.5: Tatra sudaṁ sambahulā sakkā saṅgamma samāgamma ujjhāyanti khīyanti vipācenti:
root_text.sn55.25:1.7: Ettha dāni ko na sotāpanno bhavissati.
root_text.sn55.25:1.8: Yatra hi nāma saraṇāni sakko kālaṅkato.
root_text.sn55.25:1.12: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.25:2.4: Tatra sudaṁ, bhante, sambahulā sakkā saṅgamma samāgamma ujjhāyanti khīyanti vipācenti:
root_text.sn55.25:2.6: Ettha dāni ko na sotāpanno bhavissati.
root_text.sn55.25:2.7: Yatra hi nāma saraṇāni sakko kālaṅkato.
root_text.sn55.25:3.1: “Yo so, mahānāma, dīgharattaṁ upāsako buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato, so kathaṁ vinipātaṁ gaccheyya.
root_text.sn55.25:3.2: Yañhi taṁ, mahānāma, sammā vadamāno vadeyya:
root_text.sn55.25:3.3: ‘dīgharattaṁ upāsako buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato’, saraṇāniṁ sakkaṁ sammā vadamāno vadeyya.
root_text.sn55.25:3.4: Saraṇāni, mahānāma, sakko dīgharattaṁ upāsako buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato, so kathaṁ vinipātaṁ gaccheyya.
root_text.sn55.25:4.1: Idha, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe ekantagato hoti abhippasanno—
root_text.sn55.25:4.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.25:4.6: So āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.sn55.25:4.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.25:5.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe ekantagato hoti abhippasanno—
root_text.sn55.25:5.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.25:5.5: hāsapañño javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.25:5.6: So pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti, upahaccaparinibbāyī hoti, asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, uddhaṁsoto hoti akaniṭṭhagāmī.
root_text.sn55.25:5.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.25:6.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe ekantagato hoti abhippasanno—
root_text.sn55.25:6.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.25:6.5: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.25:6.6: So tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā rāgadosamohānaṁ tanuttā sakadāgāmī hoti, sakideva imaṁ lokaṁ āgantvā dukkhassantaṁ karoti.
root_text.sn55.25:6.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.25:7.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo buddhe ekantagato hoti abhippasanno—
root_text.sn55.25:7.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.25:7.5: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato.
root_text.sn55.25:7.6: So tiṇṇaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.25:7.7: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo parimutto nirayā parimutto tiracchānayoniyā parimutto pettivisayā parimutto apāyaduggativinipātā.
root_text.sn55.25:8.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo na heva kho buddhe ekantagato hoti abhippasanno …pe…
root_text.sn55.25:8.4: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato;
root_text.sn55.25:8.7: Tathāgatappaveditā cassa dhammā paññāya mattaso nijjhānaṁ khamanti.
root_text.sn55.25:8.8: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo agantā nirayaṁ agantā tiracchānayoniṁ agantā pettivisayaṁ agantā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ.
root_text.sn55.25:9.1: Idha pana, mahānāma, ekacco puggalo na heva kho buddhe ekantagato hoti abhippasanno …
root_text.sn55.25:9.4: na hāsapañño na javanapañño na ca vimuttiyā samannāgato;
root_text.sn55.25:9.8: Ayampi kho, mahānāma, puggalo agantā nirayaṁ agantā tiracchānayoniṁ agantā pettivisayaṁ agantā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ.
root_text.sn55.25:10.1: Seyyathāpi, mahānāma, dukkhettaṁ dubbhūmaṁ avihatakhāṇukaṁ, bījāni cassu khaṇḍāni pūtīni vātātapahatāni asārādāni asukhasayitāni, devo ca na sammā dhāraṁ anuppaveccheyya.
root_text.sn55.25:10.2: Api nu tāni bījāni vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjeyyun”ti?
root_text.sn55.25:10.4: “Evameva kho, mahānāma, idha dhammo durakkhāto hoti duppavedito aniyyāniko anupasamasaṁvattaniko asammāsambuddhappavedito—
root_text.sn55.25:10.6: Tasmiñca dhamme sāvako viharati dhammānudhammappaṭipanno sāmīcippaṭipanno anudhammacārī—
root_text.sn55.25:11.1: Seyyathāpi, mahānāma, sukhettaṁ subhūmaṁ suvihatakhāṇukaṁ, bījāni cassu akhaṇḍāni apūtīni avātātapahatāni sārādāni sukhasayitāni;
root_text.sn55.25:11.3: Api nu tāni bījāni vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjeyyun”ti?
root_text.sn55.25:11.5: “Evameva kho, mahānāma, idha dhammo svākkhāto hoti suppavedito niyyāniko upasamasaṁvattaniko sammāsambuddhappavedito—
root_text.sn55.25:11.7: Tasmiñca dhamme sāvako viharati dhammānudhammappaṭipanno sāmīcippaṭipanno anudhammacārī—
root_text.sn55.25:11.10: Saraṇāni, mahānāma, sakko maraṇakāle sikkhāya paripūrakārī ahosī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.25:1.2: Now at that time Sarakāni the Sakyan had passed away.
translation_text.sn55.25:1.3: The Buddha declared that he was
translation_text.sn55.25:1.4: a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.25:1.5: At that, several Sakyans came together complaining, grumbling, and objecting,
translation_text.sn55.25:1.7: Who can’t become a stream-enterer these days?
translation_text.sn55.25:1.8: For the Buddha even declared Sarakāni to be a stream-enterer after he passed away.
translation_text.sn55.25:1.12: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. The Buddha said:
translation_text.sn55.25:3.1: “Mahānāma, when a lay follower has for a long time gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha, how could they go to the underworld?
translation_text.sn55.25:3.2: And if anyone should rightly be said to
translation_text.sn55.25:3.3: have for a long time gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha, it’s Sarakāni the Sakyan.
translation_text.sn55.25:3.4: Sarakāni the Sakyan has for a long time gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn55.25:4.1: Take a certain individual who is sure and devoted to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.25:4.5: They have laughing wisdom and swift wisdom, and are endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.25:4.6: They realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.sn55.25:4.7: This individual is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.25:5.1: Take another individual who is sure and devoted to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.25:5.5: They have laughing wisdom and swift wisdom, but are not endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.25:5.6: With the ending of the five lower fetters, they’re extinguished between one life and the next … they’re extinguished upon landing … they’re extinguished without extra effort … they’re extinguished with extra effort … they head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm.
translation_text.sn55.25:5.7: This individual, too, is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.25:6.1: Take another individual who is sure and devoted to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.25:6.5: But they don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.25:6.6: With the ending of three fetters, and the weakening of greed, hate, and delusion, they’re a once-returner. They come back to this world once only, then make an end of suffering.
translation_text.sn55.25:6.7: This individual, too, is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.25:7.1: Take another individual who is sure and devoted to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.25:7.5: But they don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.25:7.6: With the ending of three fetters they’re a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.25:7.7: This individual, too, is exempt from hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They’re exempt from places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.25:8.1: Take another individual who isn’t sure or devoted to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.25:8.4: They don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.25:8.6: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn55.25:8.7: And they accept the teachings proclaimed by the Realized One after deliberating them with a degree of wisdom.
translation_text.sn55.25:8.8: This individual, too, doesn’t go to hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They don’t go to places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.25:9.1: Take another individual who isn’t sure or devoted to the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.25:9.4: They don’t have laughing wisdom or swift wisdom, nor are they endowed with freedom.
translation_text.sn55.25:9.6: the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn55.25:9.7: And they have a degree of faith and love for the Buddha.
translation_text.sn55.25:9.8: This individual, too, doesn’t go to hell, the animal realm, and the ghost realm. They don’t go to places of loss, bad places, the underworld.
translation_text.sn55.25:10.1: Suppose there was a barren field, a barren ground, with uncleared stumps. And you had seeds that were broken, spoiled, weather-damaged, infertile, and ill kept. And the heavens didn’t provide enough rain.
translation_text.sn55.25:10.2: Would those seeds grow, increase, and mature?”
translation_text.sn55.25:10.4: “In the same way, take a teaching that’s badly explained and badly propounded, not emancipating, not leading to peace, proclaimed by someone who is not a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.sn55.25:10.5: This is what I call a barren field.
translation_text.sn55.25:10.6: A disciple remains in such a teaching, practicing in line with that teaching, practicing it properly, living in line with that teaching.
translation_text.sn55.25:10.7: This is what I call a bad seed.
translation_text.sn55.25:11.1: Suppose there was a fertile field, a fertile ground, well-cleared of stumps. And you had seeds that were intact, unspoiled, not weather-damaged, fertile, and well-kept. And there’s plenty of rainfall.
translation_text.sn55.25:11.3: Would those seeds grow, increase, and mature?”
translation_text.sn55.25:11.5: “In the same way, take a teaching that’s well explained and well propounded, emancipating, leading to peace, proclaimed by someone who is a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.sn55.25:11.6: This is what I call a fertile field.
translation_text.sn55.25:11.7: A disciple remains in such a teaching, practicing in line with that teaching, practicing it properly, living in line with that teaching.
translation_text.sn55.25:11.8: This is what I call a good seed.
translation_text.sn55.25:11.9: Why can’t this apply to Sarakāni?
translation_text.sn55.25:11.10: Mahānāma, Sarakāni the Sakyan fulfilled the training at the time of his death.”

variant_text.sn55.25:10.1: dubbhūmaṁ → dubbhūmiṁ (bj, sya-all); dubbhūmi (pts1ed) | asukhasayitāni → asukhāpassayitāni (mr) | ca na sammā → devo pana sammā (sya-all, km); devo na sammā (mr)
variant_text.sn55.25:11.1: sukhettaṁ → sukkhettaṁ (sya-all), devo cassa (sya-all, km, mr)
